_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard, though New York is the lightest record in the project by nature: an overseas team that plays only two championship games a year (one guaranteed Connacht game, one guaranteed Tailteann Cup preliminary quarter-final). Both 2026 games were heavy defeats. Scorer detail recorded honestly where found.
_meta.verification: Both scorelines verified against the official Connacht GAA and RTE results tables and primary match reports (the42, gaa.ie, Irish News). The full Roscommon scorer list is sourced from the opposite side; New York's own standout scorers are named where coverage permits.

manager.name: Ronan McGinley
manager.note: Errigal Ciaran (Tyrone) man, New York resident since 2011 and a former New York captain as a player. Manager since 2024, in his second full season in charge. Still seeking his first senior championship or Tailteann Cup win on Irish soil. Publicly advocated for New York's inclusion in NFL Division 4 to give the team more competitive games.

source.snippet: The Errigal Ciaran man represented their senior football team until 2017, captaining the side along the way before linking up again as coach and, for the past two seasons, as manager.

league.note: New York does not compete in the Allianz National Football League; they sit outside the NFL hierarchy entirely, a fact McGinley has publicly campaigned to change. Their only competitive fixtures are the two guaranteed championship games.

source.snippet: They remain outside the National Football League hierarchy, although that it is something they would like to see changed moving forward.
Preliminary Quarter-Final 6 Jun Fermanagh 3-27 1-13 New York Brewster Park

Connacht GAA Championship Quarter Final Full Time in Gaelic Park New York: 1-10 (13) Roscommon: 5-22 (37). New York boosted their tally in the final quarter through three points from Meath's Frank O'Reilly and a brace from Killian Butler.

results.note: New York's guaranteed Connacht game at home in the Bronx. They actually had early scores (Jack Foley point, Jack McKenna goal) before Roscommon ran in five goals and pulled away to a 24-point win, the widest margin of the 2026 Connacht series. Partial New York scorer list (the named contributors confirmed; full breakdown not published). Roscommon's full scorer list is recorded in Roscommon's own file. Roscommon's single two-pointer (Senan Lambe) read from the same report.

source.snippet: Preliminary Quarter-Final 6 Jun Fermanagh 3-27 1-13 New York Brewster Park

results.note: New York's guaranteed Tailteann Cup game, entering at the preliminary quarter-final stage after an eight-week gap since the Connacht game. A 23-point defeat away to Fermanagh ended their season. This game is also recorded in Fermanagh's file from the opposite perspective. Full New York scorer list not sourced.

collection_meta.collected_by: Claude (research session, not yet hand-verified by Rory)
fields_not_found: Full New York scorer lists for both games (standout contributors named for the Roscommon game; nothing itemised for the Fermanagh game)
New York squad composition detail beyond the players named in match coverage
collection_meta.notes: New York is the lightest record in the project and appropriately so: two guaranteed games, both heavy defeats (24 and 23 points), no league football. The genuine story here is contextual rather than on-field — manager Ronan McGinley's public argument that New York needs more competitive games (ideally NFL Division 4 inclusion) to be viable long-term, and the recurring question over whether the Connacht championship fixture survives. The recurring on-field names are Frank O'Reilly and Killian Butler. New York's only championship win in 27 years remains the 2023 victory over Leitrim. For the book, New York and London together are best handled as a short combined 'overseas' capsule rather than two full county chapters.
